LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS BRENT & FIOL LLP, DAVID FIOL, JOSEPH BRENT'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR. THE BUCHANAN DECLARATION DEMONSTRATES THAT THERE HAS BEEN NO MEET AND CONFER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 430.41. PLAINTIFF IS IMMEDIATELY ORDERED TO RESPOND IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE TO THE MATTERS RAISED IN DEFENDANT'S MEET AND CONFER LETTER. DEFENDANT'S RESPONSE TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY JUNE 9, 2023. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; PROCEEDINGS NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

Marthe Schreiber, in court, Plaintiff in propria persona.

RE: DEFENDANT BRENT & FIOL LLP, DAVID FIOL, JOSEPH BRENT's Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 128.7.

At the outset, the court recognizes the untimely opposition filed by Plaintiff but allows oral argument from each side. Ruling is argued. At the conclusion of argument, the subject motion is taken under submission. Counsel requests that the court consider adding a specific monetary sanction amount to the proposed order and states the amount being requested, in open court. The court shall consider counsel's request.

Defendants David Fiol, Joseph Brent and Brent & Fiol, LLP's motion for sanctions pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 128.7 is continued on the court's own motion to November 16, 2023, at 9:30 am in this department to be heard with Defendants' demurrer to Plaintiff's amended complaint.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-07-2023 Continued To Nov-01-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-06-2023 Continued To Aug-30-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendants For Sanctions Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 128.7
Off Calendar Jul-13-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:

Marthe Schreiber, in person, (415) 309-5404, in propria persona Plaintiff.

ALISON P. BUCHANAN, Esq., (via Zoom) HOGE, FENTON, JONES & APPEL, INC., (408) 287-9501, for Defendants.

Hearing RE: Defendants BRENT & FIOL LLP, DAVID FIOL and JOSEPH BRENT's Motion for Sanctions Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Section 128.7.

Ruling - ARGUED. At conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants' motion for CCP 128.7 sanctions is denied without prejudice. As ruled today on defendants' demurrer, the amended complaint is unintelligible. It must be further amended before the court can determine whether its claims are "frivolous and improper."

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-13-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:

Marthe Schreiber, in person, (415) 309-5404, in propria persona Plaintiff.

ALISON P. BUCHANAN, Esq., (via Zoom) HOGE, FENTON, JONES & APPEL, INC., (408) 287-9501, for Defendants.

Hearing RE: Defendants BRENT & FIOL LLP, DAVID FIOL and JOSEPH BRENT's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Ruling - ARGUED. Plaintiff requests sixty (60) days to amend her complaint. The court confirms that it is allowing her thirty (30) days. Counsel for Defendant offers advocacy re the court's tentative ruling on defendant's demurrer to the amended complaint. At conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The complaint is unintelligible. (CCP 430.10(f); see also Memo. 12:25-13:13.) It starts with a check-box caption page that names only one of the apparent defendants. This is followed by two other check-box pages - both only partially completed. The first contains a garbled "other" paragraph; the second, a handwritten "Did not perform fiduciary duties," though such a cause of action is not asserted. There follow 40-some pages of unorganized documents.

Counsel for defendant is instructed to prepare and submit a proposed order which should include language regarding sustaining defendant's demurrer with leave to amend and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-21-2023 Continued To Jul-13-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendants For Sanctions Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 128.7

MINI MINUTES:

Defendants BRENT & FIOL LLP, DAVID FIOL and JOSEPH BRENT's unopposed motion for sanctions per CCP 128.7.

Appearances:

Marthe Schreiber, in court, in propria persona, Plaintiff.

ALISON P. BUCHANAN, Esq., HOGE, FENTON, JONES & APPEL, INC., (408) 287-9501, for Defendants DAVID FIOL, JOSEPH BRENT, and BRENT & FIOL, LLP.

The court at the outset recognizes that Defendants have a Demurrer to the First Amended Complaint set in this matter for Jun-29-2023 and suggests continuing that matter and the matter on today's calendar to Jul-13-2023 so that both matters may be concurrently adjudicated. No argument is provided to the court's suggestion.

Defendants BRENT & FIOL LLP, DAVID FIOL and JOSEPH BRENT's unopposed motion for sanctions per CCP 128.7 is continued in open court to Jul-13-2023 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 302. Defendants BRENT & FIOL LLP, DAVID FIOL and JOSEPH BRENT's Demurrer to the First Amended Complaint set in this matter for Jun-29-2023, is continued in open court to the same date and time.

Judge: Richard B, Ulmer Jr., Clerk: R. Woods, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-09-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BRENT & FIOL LLP, DAVID FIOL, JOSEPH BRENT DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT

No appearance.

The court adopted its tentative ruling, as follows:

DEFENDANTS BRENT & FIOL LLP, DAVID FIOL, JOSEPH BRENT'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR. The Buchanan declaration demonstrates that there has been no meet and confer in compliance with CCP 430.41. Plaintiff is immediately ordered to respond in person or by telephone to the matters raised in defendant's meet and confer letter. Defendant's response to the amended complaint is now due by June 9, 2023.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported. (302/RBU)
